dimensions.label: Formatting Quality
dimensions.reason: Sections are readable but the resume is short and has no dedicated Experience section.
dimensions.tip: Add an Experience (or Internships) section and keep date formats consistent.

dimensions.label: Impact Metrics
dimensions.reason: None of your bullets contain quantified results.
dimensions.tip: Add numbers — e.g. 'built a 5-page site' or 'handled 100+ records'.

dimensions.label: Skills Coverage
dimensions.reason: Only 2 of 6 required Backend Developer skills are present.
dimensions.tip: Learn and list Node.js, Express.js, REST APIs and MongoDB.

dimensions.label: Keyword Coverage
dimensions.reason: Few backend keywords (API, server, database, CRUD) appear in the resume.
dimensions.tip: Describe projects using role keywords like REST API, schema and JWT.

dimensions.label: Project Quality
dimensions.reason: Your Portfolio Website is a real, relevant project — but it's a single, frontend-only piece.
dimensions.tip: Add one backend project (a Node.js + Express REST API) to show server skills.

strengths: Solid JavaScript fundamentals (a verified required skill).
Has a real, shipped project: the Portfolio Website.
weaknesses: Missing 4 of 6 required backend skills: Node.js, Express.js, REST APIs, MongoDB.
No internship or professional experience listed (experience 0/20).
No quantified achievements on the resume.
nextActions: Start Node.js (roadmap Week 1-4) — it unlocks Express, REST APIs and your backend projects.
Build the roadmap's notes/task REST API and ship it publicly on GitHub.
Add any real experience — an internship, freelance gig, or open-source contribution.

roadmap.project: Build a small Node.js server with one route
roadmap.resource: Node.js docs + a Node crash course

roadmap.project: Turn your server into an Express app with multiple routes
Build a REST API for a notes or task app
Persist the notes/task API in MongoDB (full CRUD)

dimensions.label: Formatting Quality
dimensions.reason: All four sections are present with clear contact details.
dimensions.tip: Keep it tight to one page and maintain consistent date formats.

dimensions.label: Impact Metrics
dimensions.reason: Every bullet is quantified (30% faster, 1,200+ users, 500+ records).
dimensions.tip: Maintain this — lead each bullet with the metric where possible.

dimensions.label: Skills Coverage
dimensions.reason: All 6 of 6 required Backend Developer skills are present.
dimensions.tip: Add nice-to-haves like Docker to broaden your profile further.

dimensions.label: Keyword Coverage
dimensions.reason: Strong technical content but a few role keywords are missing.
dimensions.tip: Weave in keywords like schema, middleware and endpoint where relevant.

dimensions.label: Project Quality
dimensions.reason: Two relevant backend projects with clear tech stacks and summaries.
dimensions.tip: Add a short architecture note or system-design detail to stand out.

strengths: All 6 required Backend Developer skills are present (100% coverage).
Bonus nice-to-haves matched: SQL, Authentication, Unit Testing.
Strong, quantified projects including an E-commerce API.
weaknesses: A few role keywords could be added for keyword coverage.
nextActions: Apply now — you're interview-ready for Backend Developer.
Prepare for technical interviews: system design and data structures.
Pursue an internship or contract role to deepen professional experience.
